goal: Allow cxs-switch to target a specified dev checkout

summary.title: Allow cxs-switch to target a specified dev checkout
summary.what: Updated scripts/cxs-switch.sh so dev/status can target an explicit cxs checkout or worktree via --repo <path> or CXS_SWITCH_REPO, while preserving the existing default of using the script repo.
summary.why: The old switcher could only point global cxs and skill slots at the checkout containing the script, which made it awkward to test isolated worktrees such as the session-sources workflow without manually rewriting the global shim and skill symlinks.
summary.user_goal: Allow cxs-switch to target a specified dev checkout

decisions.point: How to choose the dev checkout
decisions.chose: Add --repo and CXS_SWITCH_REPO, defaulting to the script checkout when omitted.
decisions.rationale: This preserves the current common path while making isolated worktree testing explicit and repeatable.
rejected: Require manual shim edits for each isolated worktree

decisions.point: How status reports mixed dev targets
decisions.chose: Report dev-other when the global shim or skill symlink points at a different checkout than the selected target repo.
decisions.rationale: The exact failure mode in this task was believing dev mode was active while it actually targeted the wrong checkout.
rejected: Only print dev/release without the target path

review_notes: Verification passed: bash -n scripts/cxs-switch.sh and git diff --check.
Verified status default reports /Users/envvar/work/repos/cxs and status --repo reports dev-other for the session-sources worktree before switching.
Used the new --repo option to switch global cxs and both cxs skill slots to /Users/envvar/.config/superpowers/worktrees/cxs/session-sources-workflow.
Global cxs now shows --source in status help and E1 artifact find smoke passes.
Mainline preflight reported broad proposed overlaps from existing branch-vs-main state, but the only dirty/current commit file for this intent was scripts/cxs-switch.sh.

architectural_claims: cxs-switch can now target any local cxs checkout/worktree for dev mode without editing the script itself.
behavioral_changes: status output shows dev target paths and dev-other when the selected target differs from the active global target.
dev mode validates the target checkout has package.json and skill-packages/cxs before switching.
